name: Landscaping from Benavente to Morales del Río — Benavente y los Valles
name_original: Paisajismo de Benavente a Morales del Río — Benavente y los Valles
region_intro: Touring cycling around Benavente, Spain, offers diverse landscapes characterized by open fields, natural reserve lagoons, and picturesque river valleys. The region features routes along the Tera and Esla River Valleys, providing scenic paths for cyclists. The terrain includes areas like the Lagoons of Vilafáfila, known for birdwatching and clear skies, making it suitable for longer road cycling routes. Benavente's surroundings offer a mix of flat sections and gentle undulations, catering to various fitness levels. Best touring cycling routes around Benavente The most popular touring cycling route is Landscaping from Benavente to Morales del Río — Benavente y los Valles, a difficult 30.2 miles (48.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 22 minutes to complete, exploring the varied landscapes of the Benavente y los Valles region. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vía de la Plata Greenway Loop, an easy 10.2 miles (16.4 km) path. This route follows a section of the historic Vía de la Plata, offering a generally flat and accessible ride. Local touring cyclists also love the Rodeando el río Esla desde Benavente — Benavente, a 12.7 miles (20.5 km) trail leading through the Esla River Valley, often completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes. Touring cycling around Benavente is defined by river valleys, open fields, and sections of historic greenways, offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Benavente are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 18 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Benavente's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Benavente?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Benavente, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 5 easy routes, 8 moderate routes, and 8 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in Benavente?
A: Touring cycling in Benavente, Spain, features diverse landscapes including open fields, natural reserve lagoons, and picturesque river valleys. You'll find routes along the Tera and Esla River Valleys, with a mix of flat sections and gentle undulations. The area is generally well-suited for cycling, with many routes being well-paved.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Benavente?
A: Yes, Benavente offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383208256/via-de-la-plata-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 16.4 km path that follows a generally flat section of the historic Vía de la Plata. Another accessible option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383246378/rodeando-el-rio-esla-desde-benavente-benavente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rodeando el río Esla desde Benavente&lt;/a&gt;, a 20.5 km trail through the Esla River Valley.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see along the cycling routes?
A: The region around Benavente is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can cycle near the Lagoons of Vilafáfila, known for birdwatching, or explore routes along the Tera and Esla River Valleys. Within Benavente itself, you can visit the historic town center, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María del Azogue&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy views from the Gardens of La Mota. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge of the Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2810181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Esla River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Benavente?
A: Yes, there are circular touring cycling routes available. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036970854/alija-del-infantado-from-benavente-circular-via-arrabalde-via-verde-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alija del Infantado from Benavente – circular via Arrabalde – Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 67.4 km route that offers a comprehensive exploration of the area.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Benavente?
A: The touring cycling routes around Benavente are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 18 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from open fields to scenic river valleys, and the well-maintained paths that cater to different ability levels. The blend of natural beauty and historical points of interest also contributes to a rewarding experience.

Q: Is there parking available near the touring cycling routes?
A: While specific parking areas for each route are not detailed, Benavente, as a town, offers various parking options. For routes starting directly from the town, you can typically find parking within the urban area or at designated spots near trailheads. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Can I access touring cycling routes in Benavente using public transport?
A: Benavente is a well-connected town, and public transport options like buses are available, which may allow for access to the general area. However, specific information on transporting bicycles via public transport to trailheads is not readily available and would require checking with local transport providers directly.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Benavente?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and comfortable conditions. Summer can be quite warm, especially in open fields, while winter might bring cooler temperatures. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, experienced touring cyclists will find challenging routes around Benavente. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383991697/landscaping-from-benavente-to-morales-del-rio-benavente-y-los-valles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Landscaping from Benavente to Morales del Río — Benavente y los Valles&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 48.7 km trail that explores varied landscapes and offers a significant elevation gain, providing a good test for more advanced riders.

Q: What are the Lagoons of Vilafáfila, and can I cycle near them?
A: The Lagoons of Vilafáfila are a significant natural reserve located approximately 27 km from Benavente. This area is renowned for birdwatching and its clear skies. While the guide doesn't list a specific route directly to the lagoons, the region's characteristics suggest that road cycling tours around the Villafáfila and Villalpando Natural Reserve Lagoons are popular among local cyclists, often covering distances of over 100 km.
tips: A small town in the northeast of Zamora near the Sierra de Carpurias and the Eria river.

Benavente is a crossroads of both roads and rivers. Its settlement has given shelter to pilgrims, troops and even Napoleon who, on his way to Astorga, lodged his troops leaving an unforgettable trail of destruction. It also gives way to the fields of Zamora, leaving León behind. Its monumental buildings testify to the importance of its location in the passage of history. It is an ideal place, with all the services, to make a stop along the way.
In the historic center, the Church of Santa María del Azogue, from the 16th century, and in Gothic style, stands out as the main monument of Benavente.

Castle-fortress from the 12th century located on top of a hill overlooking the surrounding landscape. Since 1972 it has been owned by the Parador Nacional de Turismo group and functions as a vacation hotel.
